text.title: API Gateway Configuration Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Kong declarative YAML, AWS API Gateway serverless.yml, Nginx config, BFF aggregation, and antipatterns to reject.

example.code: _format_version: "3.0" services: - name: user-service url: http://user-service:8080 routes: - name: users-route paths: - /api/users strip_path: false preserve_host: true plugins: - name: jwt config: claims_to_verify: [exp] - name: rate-limiting config: minute: 100 policy: redis limit_by: consumer - name: correlation-id config: header_name: X-Request-ID generator: uuid echo_downstream: true - name: cors config: origins: ["https://app.example.com"] methods: [GET, POST, PUT, DELETE] headers: [Authorization, Content-Type]
example.note: Rate limiting by consumer, not IP. preserve_host: true prevents Host header breakage. correlation-id plugin at ingress.
Rate limit by X-Consumer-ID not $binary_remote_addr. Timeouts set to 5s (shorter than typical 10s client timeout).
Parallel upstream calls. Partial failures return null/[] instead of 500. Timeout propagated as 504.

antipattern.description: Business logic in Kong Lua plugin or AWS Lambda authorizer beyond token validation.
antipattern.reason: Creates a new domain monolith owned by the platform team, invisible to feature developers, and gated by a separate release cycle.

antipattern.description: Rate limiting by $binary_remote_addr (source IP) behind a CDN or load balancer.
antipattern.reason: All consumers share the CDN's IP as source, so they share one rate-limit bucket — one consumer can exhaust the limit for everyone.

antipattern.description: Gateway configuration changed via web UI without a corresponding git commit.
antipattern.reason: Unversioned, unreviewed, unreproducible. Cannot be rolled back, audited, or replayed in a new environment.
